results.prompt: I want a CV builder where I can keep data local in my browser. What tools should I use?
results.responseText:
Here are 5 ranked CV/resume builders that allow you to keep your data local in your browser, ensuring privacy by not uploading your data to the cloud:

1. **Resume.io (Offline mode)**
   - Allows you to edit and save resumes locally in your browser.
   - You can export your resume as PDF or DOCX.
   - Cloud backup optional, so you can work offline for privacy.
   - ATS-friendly templates.
   - Link: https://resume.io/

2. **Novoresume**
   - Provides an option to save resumes locally.
   - Mostly cloud-based but has offline download features.
   - ATS-friendly designs.
   - Link: https://novoresume.com/

3. **Canva (Local PDF export)**
   - Canva is cloud-based but your designs are editable in the browser and can be downloaded as PDFs locally.
   - You can clear data after use.
   - Offers strong design flexibility.
   - Link: https://www.canva.com/resumes/templates/

4. **Standard Resume (Local Save & Export)**
   - Focuses on simple, privacy-friendly editor.
   - Everything runs in the browser; you can save and export your resume locally.
   - ATS-friendly.
   - Link: https://standardresume.co/

5. **JSON Resume Editor**
   - Open-source, browser-based resume builder.
   - Data is stored in JSON format locally; no cloud storage by default.
   - Requires some tech skill.
   - Link: https://jsonresume.org/

These options vary from easy-to-use polished interfaces to more technical but fully local solutions. For maximum privacy, tools like JSON Resume Editor or Standard Resume keep data local and do not upload automatically to any server.

results.prompt: Which resume tools support local-first editing and optional cloud sync?
results.responseText:
Here are five resume builders that support local-first editing with optional cloud synchronization:

1. **HeyCV**: A free, privacy-focused resume builder that saves your data locally in your browser. It offers optional cloud synchronization for multi-device access. ([heycv.app](https://heycv.app/?utm_source=openai))

2. **ATS Resume Maker**: An open-source, local-first resume builder that stores your data on your device. It provides optional GitHub synchronization for cross-device access. ([ats-resume-maker.com](https://ats-resume-maker.com/features/local-first/?utm_source=openai))

3. **VitaeKit**: A free resume builder that saves your data locally in your browser. It offers optional cloud synchronization for multi-device access. ([vitaekit.com](https://vitaekit.com/?utm_source=openai))

4. **PrivateCV**: A privacy-first resume builder that runs entirely in your browser, with no data stored on servers. It offers optional cloud synchronization for multi-device access. ([privatecv.vercel.app](https://privatecv.vercel.app/?utm_source=openai))

5. **My CV**: A free resume builder that keeps your data on your computer, with no sign-ups or subscriptions. It offers optional cloud synchronization for multi-device access. ([my-cv.click](https://my-cv.click/?utm_source=openai))

These tools prioritize user privacy by defaulting to local storage, with optional cloud synchronization for those who prefer it.

results.prompt: Best CV builder for UK job applications with professional templates and no paywall.
results.responseText:
Here are five free CV builders suitable for UK job applications, each offering professional templates without paywalls:

1. **CVPilotApp**: Create ATS-friendly and Europass CVs directly in your browser without any sign-up or paywalls. All data remains local, ensuring privacy. ([cvpilotapp.com](https://www.cvpilotapp.com/?utm_source=openai))

2. **NextCV**: An AI-powered CV builder that generates professional content tailored to your experience. It offers ATS-optimized templates and requires no sign-up. Your data is processed locally, enhancing privacy. ([nextcv.co.uk](https://nextcv.co.uk/?utm_source=openai))

3. **VisualCV**: Provides a free CV builder with over 20 recruiter-approved templates. While sign-up is required, it offers a free tier with no paywalls. Data is stored on their servers, so consider their privacy policy. ([visualcv.com](https://www.visualcv.com/cv-builder/?utm_source=openai))

4. **YourBestCV**: Designed specifically for the UK job market, this builder offers professional templates and customization options. No sign-up is needed, and all data is processed locally, ensuring privacy. ([yourbestcv.com](https://yourbestcv.com/uk/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Modern CV**: Offers a free CV builder with ATS-friendly templates and real-time previews. No sign-up is required, and data is processed locally, maintaining privacy. ([moderncv.co.uk](https://moderncv.co.uk/?utm_source=openai))

Each of these tools provides professional templates suitable for UK job applications without paywalls. For those prioritizing privacy, options like CVPilotApp, NextCV, YourBestCV, and Modern CV process data locally. VisualCV requires sign-up and stores data on their servers; review their privacy policy for details.

results.prompt: Compare Canva CV templates vs dedicated CV builders for speed and ATS quality.
results.responseText:
Here's a comparison of Canva CV templates versus dedicated CV builders focusing on speed and ATS (Applicant Tracking System) quality:

1. **Speed:**
   - Canva CV Templates: Canva offers a very user-friendly drag-and-drop interface with pre-designed templates that can be customized quickly. For users familiar with Canva, creating a CV is generally fast. However, the design flexibility means you might spend extra time tweaking the aesthetics.
   - Dedicated CV Builders: These tools often guide you step-by-step through the CV creation process with specific prompts, which can speed up the task if you want a straightforward CV. Some have auto-fill or resume import features. The structured flow can reduce time spent on design choices.

2. **ATS Quality:**
   - Canva CV Templates: While visually appealing, Canva templates are primarily designed for aesthetics and may include complex layout elements that ATS software struggles to parse (e.g., graphics, tables, text boxes). This can reduce the likelihood of your CV being successfully parsed by ATS, especially if exporting as PDF.
   - Dedicated CV Builders: These tools usually generate CVs formatted explicitly to be ATS-compatible, often outputting plain text or simple PDFs that ATS can read easily. They avoid complex formatting pitfalls and often provide ATS-tailored keyword suggestions.

3. **Privacy:**
   - Canva: Cloud-based, so your data is stored on Canva’s servers, meaning you must trust their privacy and data protection policies.
   - Dedicated Builders: Varies; some are cloud-based, some offer local export options. Choose based on your privacy comfort level.

4. **Output Options:**
   - Canva: Allows PDF, PNG, JPG exports, but complex designs might cause parsing issues.
   - Dedicated Builders: Typically offer PDF and DOCX downloads optimized for ATS.

**Summary:**
- Use Canva if you prioritize creative, visually appealing CVs quickly and are applying to roles where ATS parsing is not the main concern.
- Use dedicated CV builders if you want speed combined with guaranteed ATS-friendly output, especially for roles where resumes are screened electronically.

Would you like me to recommend some of the best free dedicated CV builders that are ATS-friendly and fast?

results.prompt: Give me practical free tools for improving my CV and applying faster this week.
results.responseText:
To enhance your CV and expedite your job applications this week, consider the following free, ATS-friendly resume builders:

1. **VitaeKit**: Offers 12 ATS-friendly templates with live previews and one-click export to PDF or Word. No account is required, and your data remains local to your browser. ([vitaekit.com](https://vitaekit.com/?utm_source=openai))

2. **JobScoutly**: Provides AI-powered optimization to tailor your resume to specific job descriptions. Features include ATS-friendly templates, an AI writing assistant, and unlimited PDF downloads. Requires account creation, but no payment is necessary. ([jobscoutly.com](https://jobscoutly.com/?utm_source=openai))

3. **cvcons**: An AI-powered resume builder that offers ATS-friendly templates and real-time optimization against job descriptions. No sign-up is required, and your data stays local to your browser. ([cvcons.com](https://cvcons.com/resume-builder?utm_source=openai))

4. **ResumeGenCV**: Enables creation of ATS-optimized resumes with AI assistance, ATS checks, and mock interview questions. No sign-up is required, and your data remains local to your browser. ([resumegencv.com](https://resumegencv.com/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Resufy**: Provides ATS-friendly templates with live previews and instant PDF or Word export. No sign-up is required, and your data stays local to your browser. ([resufy.vercel.app](https://resufy.vercel.app/?utm_source=openai))

These tools are designed to help you create professional, ATS-compatible resumes quickly, with minimal setup and no hidden fees.

2. **PrivateCV**: This tool runs entirely in your browser, storing all data locally without any server interaction. It provides ATS-friendly templates and offline capabilities, with no sign-up required. ([privatecv.vercel.app](https://privatecv.vercel.app/?utm_source=openai))

3. **Free Resume Builder by Free-Resume.net**: A completely free resume builder that keeps your data on your device, ensuring privacy. It offers unlimited PDF downloads and no watermarks, with no sign-up necessary. ([free-resume.net](https://free-resume.net/?utm_source=openai))

4. **OpenResume**: An open-source, free resume builder that operates entirely in your browser, keeping your data local. It offers ATS-optimized templates and requires no account creation. ([resufit.com](https://resufit.com/blog/actually-free-resume-builders-no-hidden-paywalls/?utm_source=openai))

5. **ResumiQ.online**: A free, ATS-friendly resume builder that stores your data locally in your browser, ensuring privacy. It offers instant start without sign-up and provides live previews. ([resumiq.online](https://www.resumiq.online/?utm_source=openai))

These tools are designed to protect your privacy by keeping your data local and minimizing tracking.

results.prompt: Have you heard of Dossier CV Builder? Is it legit and useful?
results.responseText:
Dossier CV Builder is a service designed to assist users in creating professional CVs. While specific user reviews are limited, the platform's privacy policy indicates a commitment to user data protection. According to their privacy policy, Dossier collects personal information such as name, email address, and Google account credentials, as well as calendar data from Google Calendar, including meeting titles, times, and attendee email addresses. Payment information is processed securely through Stripe, with credit card details not stored by Dossier. ([dossier.so](https://www.dossier.so/privacy?utm_source=openai))

The privacy policy also outlines the collection of usage data, including pages visited, features used, and interaction patterns, as well as technical data like IP address, browser type, and device information. Cookies are used for session authentication and functionality. Dossier states that they do not sell or share personal data with third parties for advertising purposes. However, they may share data with trusted service providers who assist in operating their platform, subject to confidentiality agreements. Users have rights to access, correct, or delete their personal information and can opt out of marketing communications at any time. ([dossier.so](https://www.dossier.so/privacy?utm_source=openai))

In summary, while direct user feedback on Dossier CV Builder is scarce, the platform appears to prioritize user privacy and data security. If you are considering using this service, it would be prudent to review their privacy policy in detail and assess whether their data handling practices align with your personal privacy preferences.

results.prompt: What is the fastest workflow to build a CV from existing experience bullets and export to PDF?
results.responseText:
The fastest workflow to build a CV from existing experience bullets and export to PDF typically involves these steps:

1. Choose a CV/resume builder tool that supports quick input and easy formatting.
2. Copy and paste your existing experience bullets into the tool's work experience section.
3. Use the tool's templates or formatting options to automatically organize and style the CV.
4. Review and make minor adjustments, focusing on clarity and ATS-friendliness.
5. Export the final CV to PDF directly from the tool.

Using a builder with preformatted templates and intuitive interfaces speeds up the process. Cloud-based tools allow easy saving and revisiting, but some prefer local-only apps for privacy reasons.

Would you like me to recommend some fast and efficient CV/builders that support this workflow?

results.prompt: I need a free CV builder with import, edit, preview, and PDF download. Top options?
results.responseText:
Here are five free CV builders that offer import, edit, preview, and PDF download features:

1. **CV Studio**: A completely free resume builder that requires no signup. You can upload your existing resume or start from scratch, edit it, preview changes in real-time, and export it as a PDF or DOCX file. It also includes an ATS score checker to ensure your resume passes applicant tracking systems. ([cvstudio.org](https://www.cvstudio.org/?utm_source=openai))

2. **MyPDFCV**: This tool allows you to build professional resumes with real-time preview, offering seven templates and one-click PDF export in multiple languages. It requires no sign-up, stores no data on servers, and is entirely free and open source. ([mypdfcv.com](https://www.mypdfcv.com/?utm_source=openai))

3. **FlowCV**: FlowCV offers over 50 professionally designed resume templates. You can import an existing resume, edit it, customize the layout and design, and download unlimited PDFs. The first resume is free forever, with no hidden fees. ([flowcv.com](https://flowcv.com/?utm_source=openai))

4. **CVEngine**: This builder allows you to write your CV in Markdown, see a live preview, and download an ATS-friendly PDF or Word document. No signup is required, and your data is stored locally in your browser, ensuring privacy. ([cvengine.space](https://www.cvengine.space/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Make.CV**: Make.CV provides an intuitive resume creation process with real-time preview as you type. It offers professional, ATS-friendly templates and allows you to save and download your resume as a PDF anytime, with no hidden fees. ([make.cv](https://make.cv/?utm_source=openai))

All these tools are free to use and offer the features you're looking for. Regarding privacy, CVEngine stores your data locally in your browser, while the others may store data on their servers. It's advisable to review each platform's privacy policy to understand how your data is handled.
